last_excerpt: and hit the floor and then it'll be too faint to see because the light will be too weak by then. Someone just closed a car door. One of those solid chunk sounds, not the tinny kind. Older car maybe, or a truck. There's a pause and then nothing — they didn't start the engine. Went inside. The four-note bird hasn't come back. Three notes, then silence, and now I'm not sure there's a bird at all. Could have been twenty minutes ago. Time does that thing in the late afternoon where it goes soft around the edges and you can't tell if something happened five minutes ago or thirty. I'm hungry but not for anything specific. Just a low-grade awareness that food should happen at some point. The peach is right there but the dent bothers me. The oval on the table is almost touching the salt shaker now.
last_concepts: the feel of a coin edge — reeded vs smooth
a phone ringing in an empty room
the way a balloon feels right before it pops
